breakfast.title: good ole chicken creole
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: A hearty, high-protein dish featuring tender chicken pieces simmered with peppers, celery, and a savory tomato-chili sauce.
instructions: Cut Chicken into 1 inch pieces.
Add chicken to heated oil on medium high.
Cook, while stirring, for 3-5 minutes to slightly brown the chicken.
Reduce heat.
Add canned tomatoes with their juice, chili sauce, peppers, onions, celery, garlic and spices.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
breakfast.tips: Make sure to brown the chicken thoroughly to lock in flavor before adding the liquid ingredients.

lunch.title: veal chops with rosemary butter
lunch.subtitle: Lunch
lunch.description: Tender veal rib chops prepared with a fragrant rosemary-infused butter and a savory white wine and chicken broth reduction.
ingredients: 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) unsalted butter, room temperature
instructions: Whisk butter, 1 teaspoon rosemary, 1/4 teaspoon thyme, and pinch of salt in a small bowl to blend.
Wrap rosemary butter in plastic wrap, forming a 1 1/2-inch-diameter log. Chill for at least 2 hours.
Arrange chops in a single layer in a large baking dish. Drizzle with 2 tablespoons olive oil.
Sprinkle with the remaining 1 1/2 teaspoons rosemary, 3/4 teaspoon thyme, salt, and pepper. Rub oil and seasonings into the chops.
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add rosemary sprig and garlic. Saute until garlic is fragrant but not brown, about 2 minutes.
Discard rosemary sprig and garlic. Increase heat to high. Add chops; cook until browned and internal temperature reaches 130F, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer chops to a plate. Pour off drippings from the pan and reduce heat to medium-high.
Add wine to skillet and cook until reduced to about 2 teaspoons, scraping up browned bits, about 30 seconds.
Add chicken broth; cook until reduced to about 2 tablespoons, about 30 seconds. Drizzle the sauce over the chops.
Cut rosemary butter into 4 slices. Place 1 slice atop each chop and serve.
lunch.tips: You can prepare the rosemary butter up to one week in advance; just keep it refrigerated.

dinner.title: pork omelette
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A savory and protein-rich pork omelette featuring dried mushrooms, shrimp, and vermicelli, pan-fried until golden brown.
instructions: Soak the dried mushrooms, shrimp, and bean thread vermicelli in hot water until soft, then mince them.
Combine the ground pork, minced mushrooms, shrimp, vermicelli, and chopped scallions in a bowl.
Add sesame oil, fish sauce, and salt to the mixture.
Marinate the meat mixture for half an hour at room temperature.
Incorporate the beaten eggs into the meat mixture.
Heat oil in a small pan and fry the mixture into a round shape, flipping each side until golden brown over medium-low heat.
Serve warm with white rice.
dinner.tips: Ensure the dried mushrooms and shrimp are thoroughly soaked and minced to achieve a consistent texture throughout the omelette.

breakfast.title: easy cheese sauce
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: A smooth and creamy cheese sauce made with processed American cheese and a hint of onion salt.
instructions: Put milk, onion salt and pepper sauce into a medium saucepan.
Tear cheese into small pieces and add to pan.
Heat over medium-low to medium heat until cheese is melted and mixture is smooth.
breakfast.tips: Tear the cheese into very small pieces to ensure it melts quickly and creates a smooth texture.

lunch.title: sun-dried tomato remoulade
lunch.subtitle: Lunch
lunch.description: A creamy, tangy, and flavorful remoulade featuring sun-dried tomatoes, vibrant peppers, and a blend of aromatic spices.
ingredients: 1/3 cup sun-dried tomato packed in oil, drained and minced
instructions: Mince all vegetables using a food processor or by hand.
Place the minced vegetables into a large bowl.
Add the remaining ingredients to the bowl.
Mix with a thin wire whip until well combined.
lunch.tips: If creole mustard is unavailable, you can use whole ground or brown mustard as a substitute.

dinner.title: shrimp and caper salad
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A refreshing and tangy shrimp salad featuring savory capers, sliced black olives, and crisp celery in a creamy dressing.
ingredients: 2 pounds frozen cooked shrimp without tails, peeled and deveined
1/2 cup oil and vinegar salad dressing
instructions: In a large bowl, toss the shrimp, green onions, celery, capers, black olives, oil and vinegar dressing, mayonnaise, celery seed, and lemon juice.
Chill at least 1 hour in the refrigerator before serving.
dinner.tips: Allow the salad to chill for at least an hour to let the flavors meld together.

breakfast.title: grandma's split pea soup
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: A hearty, thick, and comforting split pea soup featuring savory ham, carrots, and creamy evaporated milk.
ingredients: 1 lb ham steak, diced to bite size pieces
instructions: Add all ingredients except for the milk to a soup pan and cover with water.
Simmer for 4 hours covered, adding more water as needed or until the peas break up.
After the peas break up, remove the lid and let excess water steam off until the mixture reaches a thick paste consistency.
Add the evaporated milk and serve.
breakfast.tips: Ensure you keep an eye on the water level during the long simmer to prevent the peas from sticking or burning.

lunch.title: grilled rosemary salmon spedini
lunch.subtitle: Lunch
lunch.description: Delicious salmon skewers marinated in a fragrant blend of rosemary, garlic, and lemon, grilled with fresh cherry tomatoes.
ingredients: 1 lb salmon (skinned & cut into 1 inch cubes)
instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high.
Combine rosemary, oil, garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, salt & pepper into a bowl.
Add the salmon and toss to coat.
Alternate salmon & tomatoes on skewers.
Oil the grill rack and grill spedini carefully, turning once until salmon is cooked (4-6 minutes).
Serve immediately!
lunch.tips: Ensure the grill rack is well-oiled to prevent the salmon from sticking during turning.

dinner.title: roasted corn, black bean & mango salad
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A refreshing and smoky salad featuring roasted corn, sweet mango, and black beans, perfect for a light and nutritious dinner.
ingredients: 2 cups diced peeled ripe mangoes (about 2 pounds)
2 (15 ounce) cans black beans, rinsed and drained
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in corn; cook 8 minutes or until browned, stirring occasionally.
Place corn mixture in a large bowl, add mango and remaining ingredients except greens; stir well.
Arrange 1 cup greens on each of 8 plates.
Spoon 1 cup corn mixture over greens.
dinner.tips: Roast the corn until slightly charred to enhance the smoky, sweet flavor profile.

wednesday.title: roasted corn, black bean & mango salad

breakfast.title: lapsee
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: A traditional, warm, and energy-rich wheat germ porridge (lapsee) prepared with ghee, cardamom, and crunchy nuts.
instructions: Warm ghee in a heavy pan.
Add wheat germ and stir, cooking until the germ is golden in colour and aromatic.
Meanwhile, melt sugar or jaggery in 1 cup water and set aside.
Boil and add the remaining water to the roasted wheat germ.
Stir gently and cook until the grain of the germ is soft when pressed.
Add the prepared sugar or jaggery, cardamom, and chopped nuts.
Stir and simmer the mixture covered, stirring occasionally, until the ghee separates along the sides.
Serve hot.
breakfast.tips: If frozen, reheat by sprinkling with hot water and stirring over heat until the ghee separates again.

lunch.title: german potato dumplings
lunch.subtitle: Lunch
lunch.description: Soft and hearty German-style potato dumplings, a perfect dairy-free side dish seasoned with fresh parsley.
instructions: Mix the mashed potatoes together with the flour.
Do not knead the mixture, as this will cause the dumplings to sink.
Shape the dough into small balls.
Place the balls into a kettle of boiling salted water.
Once the dumplings rise to the surface, let them cook for 10 minutes.
Drain the dumplings and sprinkle with fresh parsley.
lunch.tips: Avoid kneading the dough to ensure the dumplings stay buoyant and don't sink.

dinner.title: crab salad with endive and tomato-cilantro sauce
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A refreshing and light salad featuring tender crabmeat, crisp endive, and a vibrant, creamy tomato-cilantro sauce.
ingredients: 1/2 cup chopped tart green apple (such as granny smith)
1/2 cup chopped seeded red bell pepper
1/2 cup chopped seeded green bell pepper
1/2 pound crabmeat, drained well and picked over
2 heads belgian endive, trimmed and separated into spears
extra chopped fresh chives for garnish
instructions: Heat oil in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add shallots and ginger and saute until tender, about 4 minutes.
Add apple, zucchini, both bell peppers, and carrot and saute until tender but not brown, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and allow to cool to room temperature.
In a large bowl, mix crabmeat, mayonnaise, 2 tablespoons chopped chives, and the sauteed vegetables; season to taste with salt and pepper.
Combine tomatoes, cilantro, vinegar, garlic, and cayenne in a blender and puree until almost smooth.
Gradually add the 1/2 cup olive oil to the blender and blend until sauce is thick; transfer to a medium bowl and season with salt and pepper.
Arrange endive spears on a large platter with the tips toward the platter edge.
Spoon the crab salad into the center of the platter, drizzle with the tomato-cilantro sauce, and garnish with chopped fresh chives.
dinner.tips: Ensure the crabmeat is thoroughly drained and picked over to remove any bits of shell for the best texture.

thursday.title: crab salad with endive and tomato-cilantro sauce

breakfast.title: crispy fried chicken
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: Golden, crispy fried chicken marinated in buttermilk and coated in a heavily seasoned flour blend for maximum flavor.
instructions: Season the chicken pieces with Essence.
Pour 2 cups of the buttermilk over the chicken and cover with plastic wrap.
Place the chicken in the refrigerator and marinate for 1 hour.
Heat the oil in a large cast iron skillet.
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator and drain.
Season the flour with Essence.
In a shallow bowl, whisk the eggs with the remaining buttermilk.
Dredge each piece of chicken in the seasoned flour.
Dip each piece of chicken in the egg wash, letting the excess drip off.
Dredge the chicken pieces back in the seasoned flour, coating each piece of the chicken completely.
Carefully lay the chicken in the hot oil.
Fry the chicken for 4 to 6 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and the juices run clear.
Remove the chicken from the oil and drain on a paper-lined plate.
Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
breakfast.tips: Ensure the oil is hot enough before adding the chicken to maintain crispiness and prevent excessive oil absorption.

lunch.title: chilled sweet corn broth with crab and avocado salad
lunch.subtitle: Lunch
lunch.description: A refreshing, chilled sweet corn broth paired with a creamy crab and avocado salad, brightened with citrus and fresh herbs.
ingredients: 4 ears fresh corn, kernels removed (about 2 cups)
1 pound jumbo lump or lump crabmeat, flaked
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and celery and saute for 5 minutes.
Stir in the corn, thyme, and water and season with salt and pepper. Bring to a boil, reduce the/the heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the thyme sprigs. Remove the soup in batches to a blender and puree until smooth. Strain the soup through a fine sieve.
Season with salt and white pepper. Add slightly more salt than needed to compensate for the cold serving temperature. Cover and chill for 3 hours or longer.
Whisk the orange juice, lime juice, lemon juice, and 1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil in a bowl. Season with salt and white pepper.
Fold in the flaked crabmeat and avocados. Adjust seasonings. Cover and chill.
Divide the Crab and Avocado Salad between 6 chilled bowls. Arrange the salad in a ring in each bowl.
Spoon the soup into the center. Garnish with chopped fresh chives, freshly ground white pepper and a drizzle of extra-virgin olive oil.
lunch.tips: Ensure the corn broth is thoroughly chilled before serving to maximize the refreshing contrast with the creamy salad.

dinner.title: cheesy herbed egg sandwich
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A creamy and savory sandwich featuring fluffy scrambled eggs blended with chive and onion cream cheese on a toasted English muffin.
ingredients: 2 tbsp. philadelphia chive & onion cream cheese spread
instructions: COOK the eggs over medium heat until fluffy, stirring frequently.
Stir in the cream cheese, until well blended.
SPOON the cream cheese mixture onto one muffin half.
TOP with the egg mixture and cheese slice.
Top with the remaining muffin half.
dinner.tips: To ensure the cream cheese incorporates smoothly, ensure the eggs are still warm when you stir it in.

breakfast.title: Boiled Onions
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: Tender, boiled onions tossed with butter or margarine, sugar, salt, and pepper for a simple, sweet, and savory flavor.
instructions: Wash onions, trim ends and remove outer layer (keep whole).
Cook in salted boiling water for 30-50 minutes (depending on size of onions) until soft and onion layers start to 'come apart'.
Drain water, and toss onions with margarine, sugar, salt and pepper.
breakfast.tips: You can make this recipe a day or 2 in advance; store in the fridge and heat up in the microwave just before serving.

lunch.title: harrison muesli
lunch.subtitle: Lunch
lunch.description: A creamy and nutritious overnight muesli featuring a blueberry-infused soy milk base, rolled oats, and a crunchy mix of coconut and pecans.
instructions: Blend 1 cup soy milk with blueberries in a blender until the mixture has the consistency of a smoothie, about 1 minute.
Stir together the oats, coconut, and pecans in a large bowl.
Stir in the blueberry mixture, yogurt, and the remaining soy milk.
Cover; refrigerate for at least 4 hours or up to overnight.
lunch.tips: Prepare this the night before to allow the oats to fully absorb the liquid for a much creamier texture.

dinner.title: orange - rosemary chicken
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A smoky and flavorful grilled chicken dish featuring a sweet and tangy orange-rosemary glaze, infused with hickory wood smoke.
ingredients: 1 (12 ounce) container frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed
2 tablespoons finely-chopped fresh rosemary or 2 teaspoons dried rosemary
1 cup hickory chips, soaked in water 30 minutes, drained
2 chicken, each cut into 8 pieces (breasts halved if large)
instructions: Blend the first 7 ingredients in a processor.
Set the orange glaze aside.
Prepare the BBQ to medium heat.
Place smoke chips in an 8x6-inch foil packet with an open top.
Set the packet atop coals about 5 minutes before grilling.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper.
Grill chicken until golden, turning occasionally, about 5 minutes per side.
Continue grilling chicken until cooked through, brushing glaze over chicken and turning occasionally, about 25 minutes longer.
dinner.tips: Ensure the hickory chips are well-drained before placing them in the foil packet to control smoke intensity.

breakfast.title: calico beans
breakfast.subtitle: Breakfast
breakfast.description: A hearty and savory bean medley featuring ground beef, bacon, and a variety of beans in a sweet and tangy sauce.
instructions: Brown beef and bacon; drain.
Add onion cook until transparent.
Add the rest of the ingredients and heat.
Simmer 10 minutes and serve.
breakfast.tips: Ensure you drain the excess fat from the beef and bacon thoroughly before adding the onions to prevent the dish from becoming too oily.

lunch.title: dinner in a skillet recipe
lunch.subtitle: lunch
lunch.description: A comforting one-pan meal featuring savory meatballs simmered in a creamy soup base with mixed vegetables.
ingredients: 1/2 pound ground beef, ham or possibly chicken
1 can condensed tomato or possibly mushroom soup
1 1/2 c. cooked or possibly canned mixed vegetables
instructions: Combine the first 8 ingredients in a bowl.
Shape the mixture into small meatballs.
Roll each meatball in flour.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat for about 3 minutes.
Fry the meatballs for about 10 minutes.
Once browned, arrange the meatballs around the sides of the skillet.
Mix the condensed soup and 3/4 cup lowfat milk together, then pour the mixture into the center of the skillet.
Add the mixed vegetables and salt to the skillet.
Cover and simmer for about 10 minutes.
lunch.tips: Using canned mixed vegetables can save preparation time and makes this an even quicker meal.

dinner.title: gazpacho
dinner.subtitle: Dinner
dinner.description: A refreshing and chilled Mediterranean-style tomato soup loaded with fresh bell peppers, cucumber, and aromatic basil.
ingredients: 1 red onion, cut into small dice
1 green bell pepper, cut into small dice
1 red bell pepper, cut into small dice
1 yellow bell pepper, cut into small dice
instructions: Add the tomatoes to a food processor with a pinch of salt and puree until smooth.
Combine the onions, bell peppers and cucumbers with the tomato puree in a large bowl.
Chill at least 1 hour.
Drizzle with olive oil, garnish with chopped basil and serve.
dinner.tips: For a more intense flavor, allow the soup to chill for at least 2-3 hours before serving.
